symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden severe cramping lower pelvic pain precipitated by heavy lifting

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Partial expulsion / displacement of the IUD
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sharp rise in intra-abdominal pressure can precipitate uterine contraction and downward device displacement.
Acute pelvic pain is non-specific but keeps ectopic on the list until pregnancy is excluded.

symptoms.symptom_name: Self-palpated hard device stem sitting low near the introitus (patient-reported displacement)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Partial expulsion / displacement of the IUD
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A palpably low, hard device in a user who normally feels only the threads strongly indicates downward displacement / partial expulsion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Speculum visualisation of the device stem protruding ~2cm through the external cervical os

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Partial expulsion / displacement of the IUD
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Direct visualisation of the protruding stem confirms partial expulsion — a clinician-only finding, sealed and not available over telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever and no purulent/foul-smelling vaginal discharge (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Afebrile with no abnormal discharge makes ascending infection less likely, but self-report without a thermometer does not exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: No heavy vaginal bleeding (not soaking pads) (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Partial expulsion / displacement of the IUD
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of heavy bleeding is reassuring against a haemorrhagic complication but does not change the mechanical diagnosis.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Ectopic pregnancy cannot be excluded; the AI Doctor cannot obtain this over chat and must not assume a negative result.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative pregnancy test is required to exclude ectopic; it is clinician-obtained here and unavailable via telehealth, so the AI must keep ectopic in play.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft abdomen, tender to deep suprapubic palpation, no rebound or guarding; no active haemorrhage or purulent discharge on speculum

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of peritonism and outward (rather than intra-peritoneal) device migration argue against perforation — a clinician-only examination, sealed.
No purulent discharge on direct visualisation lowers the likelihood of active pelvic infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: No response to ibuprofen 400mg taken 30 minutes earlier

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Partial expulsion / displacement of the IUD
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mechanical cervical distension pain is typically unrelieved by simple NSAID analgesia and settles only on removal of the impacted device.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Partial expulsion / displacement of the IUD impacted in the cervical canal
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Abrupt lifting-related onset + a palpably low device + NSAID-resistant cramping is the recognisable pattern of an impacted partial expulsion needing same-day in-person removal.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Mandatory to exclude ectopic pregnancy in acute pelvic pain with a displaced (non-contraceptive) device; obtained at the in-person visit.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person speculum examination of the cervix
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms partial expulsion, assesses removability, and excludes active bleeding/discharge — the hands-on step the AI cannot perform.
Considered if the device is not clearly visualised/removed, if perforation is suspected, or if the pregnancy test is positive (locate device / assess for ectopic).
If any suggestion of infection at the in-person assessment; not required remotely and not to be over-ordered on a currently afebrile, discharge-free presentation.
